submit this tx and doesn't hold the nonce slot (relayer-submitted,
server-observed, etc.). Forwarded verbatim to

The library does not change tracking behavior based on this flag —
status transitions, retention, and unseen-for-N thresholds still
apply. Consumers SHOULD use this flag in their own
Blocks of unseen observation before TrackedTx.status flips to dropped. Default 12.

timestamp on the seeded TrackedTx. Use
when the tx was submitted before you started watching it (relayer
push carrying an original submit time, rehydrating an external
record, etc.) so the strip's age indicator reflects reality rather
than starting at zero. Default

If true, fetches the receipt at inclusion. When the receipt's

The hooks bag the consumer is already passing to sendTransactionWithHooks.
Echoed onto TrackedTx.request — used for replay/replace flows.
— replace the stuck tx with a 0-value self-send.
The stuck transaction's nonce (the strip doesn't store it).
Recipient of the self-send. Defaults to
The strip entry being replaced; its
viem WalletClient that will sign the same-nonce replacement.
Bumped EIP-1559 fees for the replacement. Must exceed the original's.
— re-send the same call at a higher fee.

— the strip doesn't

One in-flight (or recently-terminal) transaction the UI is showing.

is a string so this type doesn't need to depend on

Human-readable note for terminal-with-detail states. UIs SHOULD
prefer

so a "cancelled in wallet" or decoded error name surfaces
instead of a flat "transaction failed".

Marks a tx the consumer is only observing, not authoring — e.g. a
relayer-submitted tx whose hash arrived from outside the wallet
flow. The library does not act on this field; it's a hint for
consumer UI logic (typically: skip wiring

for these, since the consumer doesn't hold
the nonce slot and can't replace).

Optional + additive: pre-existing persisted entries rehydrate with
, which is correctly falsy. Read sites SHOULD use

so the
runtime check is unambiguous (the static type can't promise the
field is present on records written before this field existed).

Per-button visibility override. Default: each button is shown iff
the matching callback is supplied.

URL builder. When omitted, the hash renders as plain text (no
anchor) — silent graceful degradation. Provider-level default
lives at a separate
Truncation style for the displayed hash. Default: 'middle'.

Replace the default layout. Receives the four atomic primitives
pre-built — the consumer rearranges them or wraps them in their
own markup.
Shown when the visible set is empty.
Provider id to read from. Defaults to the ambient context's id.

if not configured. Required only if you want
persisted

entries to auto-resume tx-tracker watching
after a reload. Without this, persisted

entries stay
'pending' until the consumer manually re-issues
Scopes both in-memory state and storage key. Default: 'default'.
Max retained entries before eviction prunes. Default: 50.
Surfaced for storage failures, watcher errors.
How long terminals linger after settling (ms). Default: 60_000.
Pixel size of the dot. Default: 16.

Pluggable persistence contract for the tx-flight strip. The Provider
loads on mount and saves on state change (debounced ~250ms).

Built-in adapters live at

.
Custom adapters just satisfy this two-method interface.
Returns null if no entry exists for the given id.
Replace stored value. Called debounced ~250ms by the Provider.
Replace the stuck tx with a 0-value self-send at a higher fee.
. Cleared when a new one starts.
True while a replacement is in flight.
Re-send the original call at a higher fee. Resolves to the new hash.

Add a fully-formed TrackedTx. Returns the supplied id.

. Each phase fans out to the consumer's
original callbacks AND a store dispatch reflecting the new state.
Imperative read; doesn't subscribe to re-renders.
Remove an entry by id. No-op if not found.
Reactive snapshot — re-renders when state changes.
